response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior individual contributor with genuine developer-tooling depth — test frameworks, CI infrastructure, device orchestration tools, and current work on AI-agent developer tooling at a major tech company — plus mobile experience shipping iOS and Apple TV apps. However, this is a people-management role requiring 2+ years of formal management and 3+ years of technical leadership, and the resume shows no evidence of managing people, running performance cycles, or leading organizational change. The teaching and workshop background suggests mentoring aptitude, but for a Level II manager opening the management gap is disqualifying at screen; he would fit better in a senior/staff IC track on the same team.
strengths: Deep developer-tools and testing-infrastructure experience (test runners, CI/CD setup, assertion libraries, MCP code generators)
15+ years of broad engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded, currently at staff level
Strong mentoring signal through created courses, workshops, and community talks
concerns: No formal people-management experience, a hard minimum requirement (2 years)
No demonstrated organizational leadership, roadmap ownership, or team-level performance management
Resume inconsistencies (e.g., a 'Founder' entry listed under a major company for an open-source side project) weaken clarity of the career narrative

key_factors.factor: Developer tools and testing infrastructure expertise
Limited evidence of technical leadership scope
